title: Marketing-sourced deals close 25% faster this month
anchor: Current week wins average 38 days to close versus the 52-day baseline from prior 11 weeks.
connect: Seven of nine wins this week came from mid-market accounts.
body: Current week wins average 38 days to close versus the 52-day baseline from prior 11 weeks. Seven of nine wins this week came from mid-market accounts.
trace: Deal velocity improvement signal from current week marketing-sourced wins showing 14-day acceleration versus prior 11-week baseline.

title: Branded search volume doubles over six weeks
anchor: Weekly branded search climbed from 1000 to 1921, with growth in all six consecutive weeks.
connect: Direct traffic crossed organic search share on April 15th for the first time.
body: Weekly branded search climbed from 1000 to 1921, with growth in all six consecutive weeks. Direct traffic crossed organic search share on April 15th for the first time.
trace: Brand momentum signal showing 92.1% cumulative growth in branded search with corresponding shift in traffic composition.

title: Enterprise win rate jumps 8 points in Q2
anchor: Q2 enterprise wins convert at 30.8% versus the trailing four-quarter average of 22.2%.
connect: Average deal size increased to $187K compared to the $142K historical baseline.
body: Q2 enterprise wins convert at 30.8% versus the trailing four-quarter average of 22.2%. Average deal size increased to $187K compared to the $142K historical baseline.
trace: Enterprise segment performance improvement with both win rate acceleration and ASP expansion versus trailing baseline.

title: Paid social pipeline creation outpaces search by 63%
anchor: Q2 paid social generated $620K in pipeline versus $380K from paid search.
connect: This reverses Q1 performance when paid search led at $720K versus social's $410K.
body: Q2 paid social generated $620K in pipeline versus $380K from paid search. This reverses Q1 performance when paid search led at $720K versus social's $410K.
trace: Channel mix shift signal showing paid social momentum reversing prior quarter's paid search dominance.

title: Mid-market SQL volume up 50% in April
anchor: April mid-market SQLs hit 147 versus the Q1 monthly average of 98.
connect: March alone generated 101 SQLs, setting the stage for April's acceleration.
body: April mid-market SQLs hit 147 versus the Q1 monthly average of 98. March alone generated 101 SQLs, setting the stage for April's acceleration.
trace: Mid-market funnel acceleration with April volume showing 50% increase over Q1 baseline and building momentum from March.

title: ABM acceptance runs 20 points ahead of baseline
anchor: ABM MQLs convert to SDR acceptance at 61.4% versus 41.6% for non-ABM sources.
connect: April ABM volume reached 70 MQLs with non-ABM at 77.
body: ABM MQLs convert to SDR acceptance at 61.4% versus 41.6% for non-ABM sources. April ABM volume reached 70 MQLs with non-ABM at 77.
trace: ABM program effectiveness showing 20-point acceptance rate advantage with roughly equal volume distribution.

title: Event pipeline velocity compresses by 25 days
anchor: Q1 event-sourced wins averaged 46 days to close versus Q4's 72-day cycle.
connect: SaaS Connect alone delivered 12 of the 17 Q1 event wins.
body: Q1 event-sourced wins averaged 46 days to close versus Q4's 72-day cycle. SaaS Connect alone delivered 12 of the 17 Q1 event wins.
trace: Event channel improvement with significant cycle compression and SaaS Connect driving majority of event-sourced closed business.

title: Fintech segment dominates recent mid-market wins
anchor: Eight of 12 recent mid-market closed wins came from fintech companies.
connect: All 12 winning accounts run Snowflake plus dbt in their stack.
body: Eight of 12 recent mid-market closed wins came from fintech companies. All 12 winning accounts run Snowflake plus dbt in their stack.
trace: Customer pattern signal showing strong fintech vertical concentration with consistent tech stack profile across all recent wins.

title: Atlas mentions lead Beacon by 52% this month
anchor: April brand mentions hit 1840 for Atlas versus 1210 for top competitor Beacon Systems.
connect: Analyst mentions doubled to nine in the last 14 days compared to six over the prior 30.
body: April brand mentions hit 1840 for Atlas versus 1210 for top competitor Beacon Systems. Analyst mentions doubled to nine in the last 14 days compared to six over the prior 30.
trace: Brand visibility advantage over primary competitor with corresponding analyst attention acceleration.

title: Reference opt-in varies by 46 points across products
anchor: Atlas Insights customers opt in for references at 75% versus Atlas Connect's 29%.
connect: Atlas Workflow sits in the middle at 70% opt-in rate.
body: Atlas Insights customers opt in for references at 75% versus Atlas Connect's 29%. Atlas Workflow sits in the middle at 70% opt-in rate.
trace: Customer advocacy signal showing significant variance across product lines with Atlas Connect lagging substantially.

title: Digital ads spend runs $84K under budget
anchor: Q2 digital ads actual spend sits at $256K versus the $340K planned allocation.
connect: The unspent budget reallocated to SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events.
body: Q2 digital ads actual spend sits at $256K versus the $340K planned allocation. The unspent budget reallocated to SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events.
trace: Budget management signal showing under-spend in digital with strategic reallocation to event channels.

title: SDR capacity now exceeds inbound volume by 48%
anchor: Current week SDR capacity sits at 210 versus 142 inbound leads.
connect: Trailing four-week average ran closer with 206 capacity and 201 volume.
body: Current week SDR capacity sits at 210 versus 142 inbound leads. Trailing four-week average ran closer with 206 capacity and 201 volume.
trace: SDR capacity utilization showing current week under-utilization versus historically balanced four-week average.

title: Three named accounts hit high-intent threshold
anchor: Current week shows three target accounts with five or more high-intent events.
connect: Two come from the Named Accounts list and one from March ABM Add list.
body: Current week shows three target accounts with five or more high-intent events. Two come from the Named Accounts list and one from March ABM Add list.
trace: ABM engagement signal showing concentrated high-intent activity across target account lists with Named Accounts list performing.

title: Atlas Assist launch locks calendar tomorrow
anchor: Product launch scheduled for May 8th with campaign assets ready and calendar locking April 24th.
connect: Launch readiness sits ahead of the typical two-week asset preparation window.
body: Product launch scheduled for May 8th with campaign assets ready and calendar locking April 24th. Launch readiness sits ahead of the typical two-week asset preparation window.
trace: Launch coordination signal showing Atlas Assist campaign preparation tracking ahead of typical timeline.

title: Forrester and G2 drive recent analyst coverage
anchor: Last 14 days analyst mentions split between Forrester and G2 with three each.
connect: Gartner contributed one mention during the same window.
body: Last 14 days analyst mentions split between Forrester and G2 with three each. Gartner contributed one mention during the same window.
trace: Analyst relations signal showing concentration in Forrester and G2 coverage with limited Gartner presence.

title: Direct traffic claims 42% share for first time
anchor: April traffic composition shows direct at 42.3% and organic search at 36.5%.
connect: Direct traffic first exceeded organic on a cumulative basis on April 15th.
body: April traffic composition shows direct at 42.3% and organic search at 36.5%. Direct traffic first exceeded organic on a cumulative basis on April 15th.
trace: Traffic source evolution showing direct overtaking organic search as primary source mid-month for the first time.
